display.purpose: The weather verdict (Fair, Stormy) and the About page title. The single largest element on any screen.

headline.purpose: The provider name in the drill-down dialog.

title.purpose: Masthead wordmark and each provider tile's name.

body.purpose: The reading sub-line and all prose; capped near 65ch.

label.purpose: Uppercase tracked micro-labels of four words or fewer: dial scale ends, uptime-window headers, dialog kickers.

data.purpose: Every polling number: uptime %, version string, timestamps. Tabular so it never jitters.

shadows.name: hairline
shadows.purpose: Default for cards and the dialog. Barely there.

shadows.name: lift
shadows.purpose: The reading band only. Marks the headline as the focal answer.

shadows.name: ring
shadows.purpose: Inset hairline where a border can't be used.

motion.name: ease-standard
motion.purpose: Default easing for hover and state transitions.

motion.name: needle-sweep
motion.purpose: The dial needle easing to the live reading (soft ease-out, no bounce). Reduced-motion snaps it instantly.

components.name: Provider Tile
components.description: A status-aware provider tile. Color comes entirely from the --c custom property set to the live status hue.
components.html:
Cloudflare

Operational
24H
100%
7D
99.98%
30D
99.95%
90D
99.97%
components.css: .ds-tile { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 12px; border: 1px solid var(--border, #e6eaf0); border-radius: 14px; background: var(--surface, #fff); padding: 16px; box-shadow: 0 1px 2px rgb(16 24 33 / 0.05); font-family: 'Hanken Grotesk', system-ui, sans-serif; max-width: 17rem; transition: transform 180ms cubic-bezier(0.4,0,0.2,1), border-color 180ms cubic-bezier(0.4,0,0.2,1); } .ds-tile:hover { transform: translateY(-2px); border-color: color-mix(in oklab, var(--c) 45%, var(--border, #e6eaf0)); } .ds-tile__head { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 8px; } .ds-tile__led { color: var(--c); display: grid; place-items: center; } .ds-tile__name { margin: 0; font-family: 'Space Grotesk', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.95rem; font-weight: 600; color: var(--text, #18212e); } .ds-tile__pill { margin-inline-start: auto; display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 5px; font-size: 0.69rem; font-weight: 600; color: var(--c); background: color-mix(in oklab, var(--c) 13%, transparent); padding: 3px 9px; border-radius: 999px; } .ds-tile__dot { width: 6px; height: 6px; border-radius: 999px; background: var(--c); } .ds-tile__uptime { display: grid; grid-template-columns: repeat(4, 1fr); gap: 8px; margin: 0; padding-top: 12px; border-top: 1px solid var(--border-soft, #eef1f6); } .ds-tile__uptime div { text-align: center; } .ds-tile__uptime dt { font-size: 0.59rem; font-weight: 600; letter-spacing: 0.06em; text-transform: uppercase; color: var(--text-faint, #8a94a6); } .ds-tile__uptime dd { margin: 3px 0 0; font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', ui-monospace, monospace; font-size: 0.81rem; font-weight: 500; color: var(--text, #18212e); font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums; }

components.name: Status Pill
components.description: A standalone status chip. The tinted fill and text both derive from --c; shown here at major-outage.
components.css: .ds-pill { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 5px; font-family: 'Hanken Grotesk', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.69rem; font-weight: 600; color: var(--c); background: color-mix(in oklab, var(--c) 13%, transparent); padding: 3px 9px; border-radius: 999px; } .ds-pill__dot { width: 6px; height: 6px; border-radius: 999px; background: var(--c); }

components.name: Reading Band
components.description: The Almanac headline: the weather verdict, a plain sub-line, and the semicircular dial with its swept needle. A tinted glow keys off --c.
components.html:
Fair

9 of 9 providers operational.

StormyFair
components.css: .ds-reading { position: relative; overflow: hidden; border: 1px solid var(--border, #e6eaf0); border-radius: 18px; background: var(--surface, #fff); box-shadow: 0 10px 30px -14px rgb(20 40 70 / 0.14); padding: 32px; font-family: 'Hanken Grotesk', system-ui, sans-serif; max-width: 30rem; } .ds-reading::before { content: ''; position: absolute; inset: 0; pointer-events: none; background: radial-gradient(125% 130% at 11% -25%, color-mix(in oklab, var(--c) 13%, transparent), transparent 56%); } .ds-reading__top { position: relative; display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 12px; } .ds-reading__icon { color: var(--c); display: flex; } .ds-reading__weather { margin: 0; font-family: 'Space Grotesk', system-ui, sans-serif; font-weight: 600; font-size: clamp(2rem, 4vw, 2.6rem); letter-spacing: -0.025em; line-height: 1; color: var(--text, #18212e); } .ds-reading__sub { position: relative; margin: 12px 0 8px; color: var(--text-muted, #586478); font-size: 0.95rem; } .ds-reading__sub strong { color: var(--text, #18212e); font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums; } .ds-dial { position: relative; display: block; margin: 8px auto 0; max-width: 100%; } .ds-dial__needle { transform-origin: 110px 110px; transition: transform 0.85s cubic-bezier(0.22,1,0.36,1); } .ds-dial__scale { display: flex; justify-content: space-between; max-width: 260px; margin: 2px auto 0; font-size: 0.625rem; font-weight: 600; letter-spacing: 0.07em; text-transform: uppercase; color: var(--text-faint, #8a94a6); } @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .ds-dial__needle { transition: none; } }

components.name: Theme Toggle
components.description: Pill toggle that flips data-theme on <html> and persists to localStorage.
components.html: <button class="ds-toggle" type="button"><svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" width="15" height="15" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round"><circle cx="12" cy="12" r="4"/><path d="M12 2v2M12 20v2M4 12H2M22 12h-2M5 5l1.5 1.5M17.5 17.5 19 19M19 5l-1.5 1.5M6.5 17.5 5 19"/></svg>Light</button>
components.css: .ds-toggle { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 7px; cursor: pointer; font-family: 'Hanken Grotesk', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.75rem; font-weight: 500; color: var(--text-muted, #586478); background: var(--surface-2, #f6f8fb); border: 1px solid var(--border, #e6eaf0); border-radius: 999px; padding: 6px 12px; transition: color 180ms cubic-bezier(0.4,0,0.2,1); } .ds-toggle:hover { color: var(--text, #18212e); } .ds-toggle:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--brand, #0d7a99); outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Uptime Bar (90-day)
components.description: A flat strip of per-day cells colored by that day's status. The signature history viz.
components.css: .ds-uptimebar { display: flex; gap: 2px; align-items: stretch; max-width: 22rem; } .ds-uptimebar span { flex: 1 1 0; min-width: 2px; height: 30px; border-radius: 2px; }

components.name: Dialog Close
components.description: The pill close control on the provider drill-down dialog.
components.css: .ds-close { border: 1px solid var(--border, #e6eaf0); border-radius: 999px; background: var(--bg, #f3f5f8); color: var(--text, #18212e); padding: 0.35rem 0.8rem; font-family: 'Hanken Grotesk', system-ui, sans-serif; font-weight: 500; cursor: pointer; } .ds-close:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--brand, #0d7a99); outline-offset: 2px; }

narrative.overview:
Barometer is a weather instrument for the internet, and the interface is built to be read the way you read a real barometer: one glance, no deciphering. A swept needle on a Stormy-to-Fair dial gives the verdict; everything else (the per-provider tiles, the 90-day uptime bars, the drill-down) is the fine print you consult only when the needle has already told you something is wrong. The system is light-first, cool, and quiet at rest, with one warm note (the ember needle) and one piece of instrument chrome (barometric cyan). It carries a real light/dark pair driven by a manual toggle, not the OS preference.

The whole system is organized around restraint as information. Healthy is near-monochrome: ink on a faintly blue-tinted off-white, hairline borders, almost no shadow. Color is not decoration here, it is signal, so it is rationed. Each surface that reports a status sets a single --c custom property to the live status hue, and every tint on that surface (a 13% pill fill, a 45% hover-border blend, the faint glow behind the headline) is derived from --c through color-mix(in oklab, ...). Nothing is painted a fixed status color by hand. The payoff: when one provider goes red, it is the only red on the page, and the eye lands on it instantly precisely because the resting field is so calm.
keyCharacteristics: Light-first, cool neutral field with a single warm accent (the ember needle).
Status color is rationed and derived from one --c variable, never hardcoded per component.
Numbers are monospace and tabular so live-polling values never jitter.
Near-flat elevation; only the headline lifts and cards lift 2px on hover.
A real, designed dark theme (manual toggle, set before first paint), not an inverted afterthought.

rules.name: The --c Rule
rules.body: Status color is never a literal in a component. A surface sets --c to its live status hue, and every status-derived tint is color-mix(in oklab, var(--c) <pct>%, ...). Pill fills sit at ~13%, hover borders at ~45%. Hardcoding a status hex anywhere is forbidden; it breaks the single-source signal.

rules.name: The One-Warm-Note Rule
rules.body: Ember (--accent) appears on the needle and the offenders line, and essentially nowhere else. Its scarcity is what makes the needle the first thing you see. Do not reach for it as a generic highlight.

rules.name: The Honest-Neutral Rule
rules.body: Unknown is slate and maintenance is blue, both visibly outside the green-to-red severity ramp. A feed we could not read or a planned maintenance window must never borrow operational green or an outage red; the color itself says 'not counted'.

rules.name: The Tabular Rule
rules.body: Any figure that changes on poll (uptime %, provider counts, 'updated Ns ago') is rendered with font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums, and the reused data values are monospace. Numbers must not reflow or jitter as they refresh.

rules.name: The Label-Only-Uppercase Rule
rules.body: Uppercase with tracking is reserved for micro-labels of four words or fewer. Headings and section titles are sentence-case display type. Barometer does not put a tracked uppercase eyebrow above every section.

rules.name: The Flat-with-One-Lift Rule
rules.body: Surfaces are flat at rest on a 1px border. The reading band is the one element that lifts by default because it is the answer; everything else lifts only as a response to state (hover, focus). A drop shadow used for decoration is forbidden.

dos: Do drive every status color from the live --c custom property via color-mix(in oklab, ...). Pill fills at ~13%, hover borders at ~45%.
Do keep the resting state near-monochrome so a single red provider is the only saturated thing on the page.
Do render every polling number in JetBrains Mono with tabular-nums so it never jitters on refresh.
Do carry status as color plus a text label plus a distinct icon, always (WCAG: never color alone).
Do reserve uppercase tracked type for micro-labels of four words or fewer.
Do keep surfaces flat on a 1px border; let only the reading band lift by default and tiles lift on hover.
Do treat dark mode as a designed equal: brightened hues, deeper shadow alphas, set before first paint.
donts: Don't build a generic SaaS dashboard: no gradient cards, no big-hero-metric template, no endless identical icon-heading-text card grids.
Don't let it become a status-page clone. Barometer normalizes Atlassian-Statuspage-style feeds; it must not look like one of them.
Don't go alarmist red-everywhere. No red gradients, no sirens, no urgency as the resting mood. Severity is proportionate.
Don't ship a neon dev-tool dark mode. Light is the designed default; dark is a deliberate variant, not a 'looks technical' costume.
Don't hardcode a status hex in any component; that breaks the --c single-source signal.
Don't spend the ember accent as a generic highlight. It belongs to the needle.
Don't use a border-left/border-right colored stripe as an accent, background-clip: text gradient text, or decorative glassmorphism.
Don't put a tracked uppercase eyebrow above every section. Uppercase is a functional label, not a kicker.
